file.content:
name: CI

on:
  pull_request:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: ci-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  fmt:
    name: Formatting
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly
        with:
          components: rustfmt
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
      # `taiki-e/install-action` downloads pre-built binaries from each
      # tool's GitHub Releases (via cargo-binstall under the hood for
      # tools without a curated manifest). Replaces `cargo install …`
      # which compiles dioxus-cli (huge dep tree) + leptosfmt from
      # source on every cache miss — a multi-minute hit every time
      # `Cargo.lock` or the rustc toolchain changes.
      - uses: taiki-e/install-action@v2
        with:
          tool: leptosfmt@0.1.33,dioxus-cli@0.7.9
      - name: Formatting
        run: cargo xtask ci fmt

  clippy:
    name: Clippy
    needs: [fmt]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          components: clippy
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
      - uses: ./.github/actions/install-dioxus-desktop-deps
      - name: Clippy
        run: cargo xtask ci clippy

  check:
    name: Workspace Check
    needs: [fmt]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
      - uses: ./.github/actions/install-dioxus-desktop-deps
      - name: Workspace Check
        run: cargo xtask ci check

  unit:
    name: Unit Tests
    needs: [check, clippy]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
      - uses: taiki-e/install-action@nextest
      - uses: ./.github/actions/install-dioxus-desktop-deps
      - name: Unit Tests
        run: cargo xtest unit
      - uses: taiki-e/install-action@cargo-insta
      - name: Snapshot Tests
        run: cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject
        env:
          INSTA_UPDATE: no

  snapshot-change-check:
    name: Snapshot Change Check
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: read
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - name: Check for snapshot changes
        id: snap-changes
        env:
          BASE_REF: ${{ github.base_ref }}
        run: |
          SNAP_FILES=$(git diff --name-only "origin/${BASE_REF}...HEAD" | grep '\.snap$' || true)
          if [ -n "$SNAP_FILES" ]; then
            echo "has_snaps=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi
      - name: Snapshot review check
        if: steps.snap-changes.outputs.has_snaps == 'true'
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
          BASE_REF: ${{ github.base_ref }}
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
        run: |
          if ! git diff --name-only "origin/${BASE_REF}...HEAD" | grep -q 'CHANGELOG'; then
            if ! gh pr view "$PR_NUMBER" --json labels -q '.labels[].name' | grep -q 'snapshot-reviewed'; then
              echo "::error::PRs modifying .snap files must include a CHANGELOG entry or have the 'snapshot-reviewed' label"
              exit 1
            fi
          fi

  i18n-browser:
    name: I18n Browser Tests
    needs: [unit]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          targets: wasm32-unknown-unknown
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
      - uses: taiki-e/install-action@v2
        with:
          tool: wasm-pack@0.14.0
      - name: I18n Browser Tests
        run: cargo xtest i18n-browser

  dom-browser:
    name: DOM Browser Tests
    needs: [unit]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          targets: wasm32-unknown-unknown
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
      - uses: taiki-e/install-action@v2
        with:
          tool: wasm-pack@0.14.0
      - name: DOM Browser Tests
        run: cargo xtest dom-browser

  release:
    name: Release Verification
    needs: [unit]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
      - uses: taiki-e/install-action@nextest
      - uses: ./.github/actions/install-dioxus-desktop-deps
      - name: Release Verification
        run: cargo xtask ci release

  mutual-exclusion:
    name: Mutual Exclusion Guard
    needs: [check]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
      - name: Mutual Exclusion Guard
        run: cargo xtask ci mutual-exclusion

  integration:
    name: Integration Tests
    needs: [unit]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
      - uses: taiki-e/install-action@nextest
      - name: Integration Tests
        run: cargo xtest integration

  adapter:
    name: Adapter Tests
    needs: [unit]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
      - uses: taiki-e/install-action@nextest
      - uses: ./.github/actions/install-dioxus-desktop-deps
      - name: Adapter Tests
        run: cargo xtest adapter

  adapter-parity:
    name: Adapter Parity
    needs: [unit, adapter]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
      - name: Adapter Parity
        run: cargo xtask ci adapter-parity

  error-variant-coverage:
    name: Error Variant Coverage
    needs: [unit]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
      - name: Error Variant Coverage
        run: cargo xtask ci error-variant-coverage

  coverage:
    name: Coverage
    needs: [unit]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 90
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          components: llvm-tools-preview
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly
        with:
          targets: wasm32-unknown-unknown
          components: llvm-tools-preview
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
      - uses: taiki-e/install-action@nextest
      - uses: taiki-e/install-action@cargo-llvm-cov
      - name: Reclaim runner disk space
        run: |
          df -h
          sudo rm -rf \
            /opt/az \
            /opt/ghc \
            /opt/hostedtoolcache/CodeQL \
            /usr/local/.ghcup \
            /usr/local/lib/android \
            /usr/share/dotnet
          docker system prune -af || true
          sudo apt-get clean
          df -h
      - name: Install LLVM 22 for wasm coverage
        run: |
          wget -q https://apt.llvm.org/llvm.sh -O /tmp/llvm.sh
          chmod +x /tmp/llvm.sh
          sudo /tmp/llvm.sh 22
          sudo apt-get install -y clang-22 lld-22
      - name: Resolve wasm-bindgen version
        id: wasm-bindgen-version
        run: |
          version="$(
            cargo metadata --format-version 1 |
              python3 -c 'import json, sys; print(next(pkg["version"] for pkg in json.load(sys.stdin)["packages"] if pkg["name"] == "wasm-bindgen"))'
          )"
          echo "version=$version" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
      - uses: taiki-e/install-action@v2
        with:
          tool: wasm-bindgen-cli@${{ steps.wasm-bindgen-version.outputs.version }}
      - name: Configure wasm coverage clang
        run: echo "WASM_COVERAGE_CLANG=/usr/bin/clang-22" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
      - uses: ./.github/actions/install-dioxus-desktop-deps
      - name: Coverage
        run: cargo xtask ci coverage
      - name: Upload coverage diagnostics
        if: failure()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7
        with:
          name: coverage-diagnostics
          path: |
            lcov.info
            target/coverage/*.lcov
            target/wasm-coverage/**/profraw/*.profraw
          if-no-files-found: ignore
      - name: Snapshot Count
        run: cargo xtask ci snapshot-count
      - name: Upload to Codecov
        if: always()
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v5
        with:
          files: lcov.info
          fail_ci_if_error: true
          token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}

  feature-flags-core:
    name: Feature Flags — Core
    needs: [unit]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
        with:
          shared-key: feature-flags
      - uses: taiki-e/install-action@nextest
      - name: Feature Flags — Core
        run: cargo xtask ci feature-matrix-core

  feature-flags-i18n:
    name: Feature Flags — I18n
    needs: [unit]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          targets: wasm32-unknown-unknown
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
        with:
          shared-key: feature-flags
      - uses: taiki-e/install-action@nextest
      - name: Feature Flags — I18n
        run: cargo xtask ci feature-matrix-i18n

  feature-flags-subsystems:
    name: Feature Flags — Subsystems
    needs: [unit]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          targets: wasm32-unknown-unknown
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
        with:
          shared-key: feature-flags
      - uses: taiki-e/install-action@nextest
      - name: Feature Flags — Subsystems
        run: cargo xtask ci feature-matrix-subsystems

  feature-flags-leptos:
    name: Feature Flags — Leptos
    needs: [unit]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          targets: wasm32-unknown-unknown
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
        with:
          shared-key: feature-flags
      - uses: taiki-e/install-action@nextest
      - name: Feature Flags — Leptos
        run: cargo xtask ci feature-matrix-leptos

  feature-flags-dioxus:
    name: Feature Flags — Dioxus
    needs: [unit]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          targets: wasm32-unknown-unknown
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
        with:
          shared-key: feature-flags
      - uses: taiki-e/install-action@nextest
      - uses: ./.github/actions/install-dioxus-desktop-deps
      - name: Feature Flags — Dioxus
        run: cargo xtask ci feature-matrix-dioxus

  feature-flag-check:
    name: Feature Flag Check
    needs:
      - feature-flags-core
      - feature-flags-i18n
      - feature-flags-subsystems
      - feature-flags-leptos
      - feature-flags-dioxus
    if: always()
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Enforce feature matrix success
        run: |
          results=(
            "${{ needs.feature-flags-core.result }}"
            "${{ needs.feature-flags-i18n.result }}"
            "${{ needs.feature-flags-subsystems.result }}"
            "${{ needs.feature-flags-leptos.result }}"
            "${{ needs.feature-flags-dioxus.result }}"
          )
          for result in "${results[@]}"; do
            if [ "$result" != "success" ]; then
              echo "Feature flag check failed: $result"
              exit 1
            fi
          done
          echo "All feature flag jobs passed"

content.content:
1	//! Local CI pipeline runner.
2	//!
3	//! Mirrors the jobs declared in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` so that the xtask
4	//! crate is the single source of truth for CI commands. Run the full pipeline
5	//! with `cargo xci` or pick individual steps with `cargo xci check clippy`.
6	
7	pub(crate) mod feature_matrix;
8	
9	use std::{
10	    fmt::{self, Display},
11	    fs,
12	    io::{self, Read, Write},
13	    path::{Path, PathBuf},
14	    process::{self, Stdio},
15	};
16	
17	use crate::{coverage, i18n, lint, manifest, spec, test};
18	
19	const MUTUAL_EXCLUSION_GUARD: &str = "features `icu4x` and `web-intl` are mutually exclusive";
20	const LEPTOSFMT_VERSION: &str = "0.1.33";
21	const LEPTOSFMT_BASE_INPUTS: &[&str] = &["crates/ars-leptos"];
22	const DIOXUS_CLI_VERSION: &str = "0.7.9";
23	const DIOXUSFMT_BASE_INPUTS: &[&str] = &["crates/ars-dioxus"];
24	
25	/// CI pipeline steps, matching the GitHub Actions job names.
26	///
27	/// Steps are listed in pipeline dependency order for sequential local
28	/// execution. Use [`run`] to execute one or more steps with fail-fast
29	/// semantics.
30	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, clap::ValueEnum)]
31	#[value(rename_all = "kebab-case")]
32	pub enum Step {
33	    /// `leptosfmt --check ...`, `dx fmt --check ...`, and
34	    /// `cargo +nightly fmt --all --check`
35	    Fmt,
36	
37	    /// `cargo check --workspace --all-features`
38	    Check,
39	
40	    /// `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
41	    Clippy,
42	
43	    /// Unit tests for core crates.
44	    Unit,
45	
46	    /// Browser-executed wasm tests for ars-i18n's web-intl backend.
47	    I18nBrowser,
48	
49	    /// Browser-executed wasm tests for ars-dom's web feature.
50	    DomBrowser,
51	
52	    /// Release-profile compile and test smoke checks.
53	    Release,
54	
55	    /// Integration tests.
56	    Integration,
57	
58	    /// Adapter harness tests (Leptos + Dioxus).
59	    Adapter,
60	
61	    /// Compare per-component test counts between adapters.
62	    AdapterParity,
63	
64	    /// Generate coverage and check per-crate thresholds.
65	    Coverage,
66	
67	    /// Enforce per-component snapshot-count policy.
68	    SnapshotCount,
69	
70	    /// Parse every fenced Rust code block in the spec corpus and report
71	    /// any `syn::parse_file` syntax errors. Catches structural Rust bugs
72	    /// in spec snippets before they ship.
73	    SpecCompileSnippets,
74	
75	    /// Verify every `ComponentError` variant appears in tests.
76	    ErrorVariantCoverage,
77	
78	    /// Meta-step: run all five feature-matrix groups.
79	    FeatureMatrix,
80	
81	    /// Feature flags — ars-core (15 combos).
82	    FeatureMatrixCore,
83	
84	    /// Feature flags — ars-i18n (11 combos + wasm32).
85	    FeatureMatrixI18n,
86	
87	    /// Feature flags — subsystem crates (13 combos + wasm32).
88	    FeatureMatrixSubsystems,
89	
90	    /// Feature flags — ars-leptos (3 combos).
91	    FeatureMatrixLeptos,
92	
93	    /// Feature flags — ars-dioxus (4 combos + wasm32).
94	    FeatureMatrixDioxus,
95	
96	    /// Verify the mutual-exclusion compile guard for ars-i18n backend features.
97	    MutualExclusion,
98	}
99	
100	/// Pipeline profile selecting which step list to run when no positional steps
101	/// are passed.
102	///
103	/// - [`Profile::Full`] mirrors the GitHub Actions CI job exactly (20 gates,
104	///   ~25 minutes locally) and is the default for backward compatibility with
105	///   scripts that call `cargo xci` directly.
106	/// - [`Profile::Fast`] runs the curated [`FAST_PIPELINE_ORDER`] subset
107	///   (~5 minutes) intended as the routine pre-push gate. It drops the coverage
108	///   recompile, the wasm browser tests, the release-profile checks, and the
109	///   five `feature-matrix-*` groups — all of which are CI-only concerns that
110	///   re-test what `Unit` / `Integration` / `Adapter` already covered.
111	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default, clap::ValueEnum)]
112	#[value(rename_all = "kebab-case")]
113	pub enum Profile {
114	    /// Full CI parity (default). Runs every step in [`PIPELINE_ORDER`].
115	    #[default]
116	    Full,
117	
118	    /// Fast pre-push gate. Runs every step in [`FAST_PIPELINE_ORDER`].
119	    Fast,
120	}
121	
122	/// Default pipeline order when no steps are specified and [`Profile::Full`]
123	/// is selected.
124	///
125	/// `FeatureMatrix` is intentionally absent — the pipeline runs each sub-group
126	/// individually so progress is visible per group.
127	const PIPELINE_ORDER: &[Step] = &[
128	    Step::Fmt,
129	    Step::Check,
130	    Step::Clippy,
131	    Step::Unit,
132	    Step::I18nBrowser,
133	    Step::DomBrowser,
134	    Step::Release,
135	    Step::Integration,
136	    Step::Adapter,
137	    Step::AdapterParity,
138	    Step::Coverage,
139	    Step::SnapshotCount,
140	    Step::SpecCompileSnippets,
141	    Step::ErrorVariantCoverage,
142	    Step::FeatureMatrixCore,
143	    Step::FeatureMatrixI18n,
144	    Step::FeatureMatrixSubsystems,
145	    Step::FeatureMatrixLeptos,
146	    Step::FeatureMatrixDioxus,
147	    Step::MutualExclusion,
148	];
149	
150	/// Curated pipeline order when [`Profile::Fast`] is selected and no positional
151	/// steps are passed.
152	///
153	/// Covers cross-crate / workspace-level regressions and the structural lints
154	/// that gate merge, while skipping the wasm-coverage recompile, the wasm
155	/// browser tests, the release-profile checks, and the five `feature-matrix-*`
156	/// groups — those run in CI and rarely change the outcome of a local pre-push
157	/// gate when `Unit`, `Integration`, `Adapter`, and `--all-features check`
158	/// have already passed.
159	///
160	/// Power users who touched wasm or feature-gated code can opt the skipped
161	/// steps back in positionally, e.g. `cargo xci --profile fast i18n-browser`;
162	/// positional steps still override the profile.
163	const FAST_PIPELINE_ORDER: &[Step] = &[
164	    Step::Fmt,
165	    Step::Check,
166	    Step::Clippy,
167	    Step::Unit,
168	    Step::Integration,
169	    Step::Adapter,
170	    Step::SpecCompileSnippets,
171	    Step::ErrorVariantCoverage,
172	    Step::MutualExclusion,
173	    Step::SnapshotCount,
174	];
175	
176	/// Errors from CI operations.
177	#[derive(Debug)]
178	pub enum Error {
179	    /// A cargo subprocess exited with a non-zero status.
180	    StepFailed {
181	        /// The step that failed.
182	        step: Step,
183	
184	        /// The command that was run (for display).
185	        command: String,
186	
187	        /// Process exit code, if available.
188	        code: Option<i32>,
189	    },
190	
191	    /// A required tool is not installed.
192	    MissingTool {
193	        /// Human-readable tool name.
194	        tool: String,
195	
196	        /// How to install it.
197	        install_hint: String,
198	    },
199	
200	    /// IO error spawning a subprocess.

lastPrompt: I want to add https://minikin.me/blog/cargo-crap to our project here, I already installed it locally, I want it integrated in our xtask and to be used on CI. While we're at it, I want to also improve…

aiTitle: Integrate cargo-crap and optimize CI pipeline

content.text: There's a thorough inline test suite. Let me read it plus `step_name` and the run-loop so I scope the test updates correctly.

I now have a complete picture of both the xtask CI engine (Step enum, dispatch, pipeline orders, inline test suite, `cargo()` helper, coverage step producing `lcov.info`) and the GitHub Actions workflows. cargo-crap v0.2.2 takes options directly (no `crap` subcommand), consumes the merged `lcov.info` the coverage job already produces, and auto-detects repo URL/commit from GitHub env vars.

A few decisions genuinely shape the implementation. Let me confirm them with you.

questions.question: How should cargo-crap gate CI? (It scores complexity-vs-coverage; well-tested complex code scores low, so the gate mainly catches complex+undertested functions.)

options.label: Advisory first, then enforce
options.description: Land it producing GitHub annotations + a PR comment + SARIF upload to Code Scanning, but never fail CI yet. Collect real CRAP scores on the actual lcov, then flip to a hard gate in a follow-up once we know the right threshold. Safest rollout; we can't calibrate a threshold without a coverage run.
options.preview:
cargo crap --workspace --lcov lcov.info \
  --format github   # ::warning annotations
# + pr-comment artifact, + sarif upload
# exit 0 always (no --fail-above)

options.label: Hard fail above threshold
options.description: Run with --fail-above --threshold 30 (the tool default) immediately. Blocks merge on any function exceeding the CRAP threshold. Simplest enforcement, but may fail on day one if existing complex+undertested functions exist; threshold may need tuning.
options.preview:
cargo crap --workspace --lcov lcov.info \
  --fail-above --threshold 30
# non-zero exit fails the job

options.label: Regression-only (baseline)
options.description: Commit a baseline.json and run --baseline baseline.json --fail-regression. Only blocks merge when a function's CRAP score gets worse vs baseline; tolerates existing debt. Needs a committed baseline plus a documented refresh path.

questions.question: You called out removing the redundant `check` step (clippy --all-targets is a superset). How far should the removal go?

options.label: Remove entirely
options.description: Drop the Step::Check variant, run_check, the CI `check` job, and rewire dependents (unit, mutual-exclusion) onto clippy. Matches the repo's 'remove unused, no deprecation' convention. clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features already compiles everything check did, plus lints.
options.preview:
# delete: Step::Check, run_check, ci.yml `check` job
# rewire: unit.needs [clippy]
#         mutual-exclusion.needs [clippy]

options.label: Keep as manual-only step
options.description: Remove `check` from the CI workflow and both pipeline orders, but keep the Step::Check enum variant + run_check so `cargo xci check` still works for fast local iteration. Slightly less clean but preserves a quick local typecheck.
options.preview:
# ci.yml: remove `check` job
# PIPELINE_ORDER / FAST: drop Step::Check
# keep Step::Check usable via `cargo xci check`

questions.question: How aggressive should the broader CI speedup be? (Beyond removing check.)

options.label: Moderate (recommended)
options.description: Remove check; rewire independent compile jobs (release, integration, adapter, coverage, feature-flags, error/mutual) to depend on clippy instead of unit so they start in parallel with unit instead of waiting for it. clippy already guarantees compilation. lastPrompt: I want to add https://minikin.me/blog/cargo-crap to our project here, I already installed it locally, I want it integrated in our xtask and to be used on CI. While we're at it, I want to also improve…

aiTitle: Integrate cargo-crap and optimize CI pipeline

content.text:
I have everything I need. Before writing the plan, one critical thing the chosen "hard fail above threshold" mode forces me to flag:

- **`cargo crap --workspace` scores every workspace member against `lcov.info`.** `ars-derive` (proc-macro, intentionally unmeasured) and any non-instrumented source would be scored at 0% coverage (`--missing pessimistic` default) and explode the CRAP score, failing CI for the wrong reasons. So the gate needs scoped excludes, and — because we can't run the ~90-min coverage job in plan mode — a **calibration pass during implementation** to confirm real code passes threshold 30 before we lock the gate. The plan builds that in.
- **Removing the `check` job** means GitHub branch-protection "required status checks" must be updated by a repo admin, or merges will hang waiting for a job that no longer exists. I'll flag this as a required manual follow-up.

Let me write the plan.

input.content:
# Integrate cargo-crap + improve CI

## Context

We want two things in one effort:

1. **Integrate [`cargo-crap`](https://minikin.me/blog/cargo-crap)** (the CRAP metric: cyclomatic
   complexity vs. test coverage) into the xtask CI engine and GitHub Actions. cargo-crap surfaces
   functions that are both complex and undertested — the highest-risk code to modify, especially
   under AI-assisted development. It is already installed locally at `~/.cargo/bin/cargo-crap`
   (v0.2.2).

2. **Speed up CI and remove redundancy.** The `check` job is redundant — `clippy --workspace
   --all-targets --all-features` compiles a strict superset of what `cargo check` compiles, plus
   lints. And the whole pipeline serializes behind `fmt → clippy → unit` before fanning out, even
   though most fan-out jobs don't consume unit-test *results* (clippy already guarantees the code
   compiles).

**Decisions taken (user-confirmed):**
- cargo-crap gates CI with a **hard fail**: `cargo crap --workspace --lcov lcov.info --fail-above
  --threshold 30`.
- **Remove `check` entirely** (variant, `run_check`, CI job, pipeline entries; rewire dependents).
- **Moderate CI speedup**: rewire independent compile jobs from `needs: [unit]` to `needs:
  [clippy]` so they run in parallel with `unit` instead of waiting for it.

**Key facts established during exploration:**
- xtask is the single source of truth for CI steps (`xtask/src/ci/mod.rs`). CI YAML just calls
  `cargo xtask ci <step>`.
- The `coverage` step (`run_coverage`, `mod.rs:730`) already writes the merged native+wasm
  `lcov.info` to the repo root (`mod.rs:743,782`). cargo-crap consumes exactly that file and needs
  **no recompile** — it parses source + reads lcov. So cargo-crap belongs right after `Coverage`
  in the pipeline and as an appended step in the existing `coverage` CI job (zero extra compile,
  no artifact round-trip).
- `cargo-crap` v0.2.2 takes options directly (`cargo crap [OPTIONS]`, no `crap` subcommand);
  `--fail-above` controls exit code independently of `--format`; `--format github` emits
  `::warning` annotations; it auto-detects `--repo-url`/`--commit-ref` from `GITHUB_SERVER_URL`/
  `GITHUB_REPOSITORY`/`GITHUB_SHA`.
- The existing tool-integration template to mirror is `xtask/src/mutants.rs` (pinned-version const,
  `Options` struct, `preflight_*` check, `MissingTool` error, unit tests asserting the version pin
  and argument construction).
- `ci/mod.rs` has a thorough inline test suite (`mod tests`, ~line 1275) asserting pipeline
  contents, `step_name` coverage, and fast-vs-full invariants. These tests must be updated in lockstep.

## Part A — cargo-crap integration

### A1. New module `xtask/src/crap.rs` (mirror `mutants.rs`)

Centralize all gate config here as testable constants (single source of truth, same pattern as
`CARGO_MUTANTS_VERSION` / `LEPTOSFMT_VERSION`):

- `pub const CARGO_CRAP_VERSION: &str = "0.2.2";` — keep in sync with `ci.yml` and CLAUDE.md.
- `pub const CRAP_THRESHOLD: u32 = 30;` — the fail-above threshold.
- `const EXCLUDE_GLOBS: &[&str]` — files skipped at walk time. **Seed with** `crates/ars-derive/**`
  (proc-macro crate, intentionally unmeasured by llvm-cov; would otherwise score 0% coverage and
  explode CRAP). Add `target/**` defensively. Each entry carries a justification comment.
- `const ALLOW_GLOBS: &[&str]` — escape hatch for genuinely-justified high-CRAP functions
  discovered during calibration (A4). Empty initially. Each entry **must** have a comment
  explaining why (matches the repo's "named escape hatch + justification" convention, cf.
  `mutants.toml` `[[skip]]`).
- `pub enum Format { Human, Github }` + `fn format_for_env() -> Format` reading `GITHUB_ACTIONS`
  (set by Actions) → `Github`, else `Human`. Keeps CI annotations without polluting local output.
- `pub struct Options { lcov: PathBuf, threshold: u32, fail_above: bool, format: Format }`.
- `fn build_args(opts: &Options) -> Vec<String>` — **pure, testable** arg builder producing
  `["crap", "--workspace", "--lcov", <lcov>, "--threshold", <n>, "--format", <f>, ("--fail-above"),
  "--exclude", <g>..., "--allow", <g>...]`.
- `pub fn run(opts: &Options) -> Result<(), Error>` — `preflight_cargo_crap()`, then preflight that
  `opts.lcov` exists (helpful error pointing at `cargo xci coverage`), spawn `cargo` with
  `build_args`, inherit stdio, map exit code.
- `preflight_cargo_crap()` — `cargo crap --version` succeeds, else `Error::MissingTool` with hint
  `cargo install cargo-crap --locked --version {CARGO_CRAP_VERSION}`.
- `Error` enum + `Display` + `From<io::Error>` — copy the shape from `mutants.rs`.
- `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`: assert `CARGO_CRAP_VERSION == "0.2.2"`; assert `build_args` includes
  `--workspace`, `--fail-above` when set and omits it when not, the threshold, every `EXCLUDE_GLOBS`
  entry as an `--exclude`, every `ALLOW_GLOBS` entry as an `--allow`; assert `format_for_env` maps
  correctly given an injected env value (refactor to take the var so it's testable).

Register `mod crap;` in `xtask/src/lib.rs` and `main.rs`.

### A2. Wire `Step::Crap` into `xtask/src/ci/mod.rs`

- Add `Step::Crap` variant (doc: "Run cargo-crap CRAP gate over the merged lcov.info").
- `step_name`: `Step::Crap => "crap"` (`mod.rs:1207`).
- `run_step`: `Step::Crap => run_crap()` (`mod.rs:393`).
- `fn run_crap()` → `crap::run(&crap::Options { lcov: "lcov.info".into(), threshold:
  crap::CRAP_THRESHOLD, fail_above: true, format: crap::format_for_env() })`, mapping `crap::Error`
  into `ci::Error` (extend `ci::Error` with a `Crap(crap::Error)` arm + `Display`, mirroring the
  existing `Coverage`/`Lint` arms).
- Add `Step::Crap` to `PIPELINE_ORDER` **immediately after `Step::Coverage`** (`mod.rs:138`) so it
  reads the freshly merged lcov. **Do not** add to `FAST_PIPELINE_ORDER` (fast skips coverage → no
  lcov).

### A3. Update inline tests + docs in `ci/mod.rs` and `main.rs`

- `step_name_covers_all_variants` (`mod.rs:1418`): add `Step::Crap` to the `all` array.
- Add a test: `Step::Crap` is in `PIPELINE_ORDER`, sits directly after `Step::Coverage`, and is
  **absent** from `FAST_PIPELINE_ORDER` (add `Step::Crap` to the `banned` list in
  `fast_pipeline_excludes_*` since it requires coverage's lcov).
- New dev entrypoint: add a `Crap { lcov: Option<PathBuf>, threshold: Option<u32> }` subcommand in
  `main.rs` → `crap::run(... format: Human ...)` (defaults `lcov.info`, `CRAP_THRESHOLD`), and a
  `xcrap = "xtask crap"` alias in `.cargo/config.toml`. Lets contributors reproduce the gate
  locally with readable output after a coverage run (parallels the `xclippy` dev vs `ci clippy`
  split).
- Update the "all 20 gates" prose (`main.rs:20`, `mod.rs:103`) and the `cargo xci check clippy`
  example in the module header (`mod.rs:5`) to drop `check` and mention `crap`.

### A4. CI: append cargo-crap to the existing `coverage` job (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`)

In the `coverage` job (lines 218-288), after the `Coverage` step (line 269):
- Add `- uses: taiki-e/install-action@v2` with `tool: cargo-crap@0.2.2` (pinned; taiki-e falls
  back to cargo-binstall, which the blog confirms supports cargo-crap). Place it near the other
  `install-action` steps.
- Add `- name: CRAP gate \n run: cargo xtask ci crap`. It fails the job on threshold breach
  (`--fail-above`) and emits `::warning` annotations (github format via `format_for_env`). The
  existing `if: always()` Codecov upload still runs even if CRAP fails.

### A5. Calibration pass (MANDATORY during implementation, before locking the gate)

The full coverage job can't run in plan mode. During implementation:
1. Run `cargo xci coverage` locally (or the native-only subset) to produce a real `lcov.info`.
2. Run `cargo crap --workspace --lcov lcov.info --threshold 30 --format human --top 40` and inspect.
3. For every function over 30: prefer **adding tests** (lowers coverage-driven CRAP, matches the
   repo's test-first ethos); otherwise add a justified `EXCLUDE_GLOBS`/`ALLOW_GLOBS` entry. Only
   touch `CRAP_THRESHOLD` as a last resort.
4. **Surface the calibration result to the user** before finalizing — if real code can't pass 30
   cleanly, we decide together (more tests vs. allow-list vs. threshold) rather than silently
   widening the gate.

## Part B — CI speedup + remove `check`

### B1. Remove `check` from xtask

- Delete `Step::Check` variant (`mod.rs:37`), `step_name` arm (`mod.rs:1210`), `run_step` arm
  (`mod.rs:397`), `fn run_check` (`mod.rs:458`), and the `Step::Check` entry in `PIPELINE_ORDER`.
- Fix `explicit_steps_pass_through` test (`mod.rs:1287`, uses `Step::Check`) — switch to e.g.
  `[Step::Clippy, Step::Unit]`. Remove `Step::Check` from `step_name_covers_all_variants`.
- `cargo_with_format` stays (clippy still uses it). No other `run_check`-only code remains.

### B2. `.github/workflows/ci.yml` rewiring

- **Delete the `check` job** (lines 51-61).
- `unit.needs`: `[check, clippy]` → `[clippy]`.
- `mutual-exclusion.needs`: `[check]` → `[clippy]`.
- Rewire these jobs from `needs: [unit]` → `needs: [clippy]` (clippy guarantees compilation; none
  consume unit-test *results*): `i18n-browser`, `dom-browser`, `release`, `integration`, `adapter`,
  `error-variant-coverage`, `coverage`, `feature-flags-{core,i18n,subsystems,leptos,dioxus}`.
- `adapter-parity.needs`: `[unit, adapter]` → `[clippy]` (it only counts test files on disk).
- Net graph: `fmt → clippy → { unit, release, integration, adapter, adapter-parity,
  error-variant-coverage, mutual-exclusion, i18n-browser, dom-browser, coverage(+crap),
  feature-flags-* → feature-flag-check } `. Removes one full compile stage from the critical path.
- `feature-flag-check` aggregator unchanged.

### B3. Required manual follow-up (flag to user; cannot be done in-repo)

Removing the `check` job means GitHub **branch protection → required status checks** must drop
`Workspace Check` (job name "Workspace Check"), or PR merges will block waiting on a job that no
longer reports. This is a repo-admin action in GitHub settings, not a file change. Call it out in
the PR description.

## Critical files

- `xtask/src/crap.rs` — **new** module (mirror `xtask/src/mutants.rs`).
- `xtask/src/lib.rs`, `xtask/src/main.rs` — register module, add `Crap` subcommand, update prose.
- `xtask/src/ci/mod.rs` — add `Step::Crap` + `run_crap` + `Error::Crap`; remove `Step::Check` +
  `run_check`; update `PIPELINE_ORDER`, `step_name`, `run_step`, and the inline `mod tests`.
- `.cargo/config.toml` — add `xcrap = "xtask crap"`.
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — remove `check` job; rewire `needs:`; append cargo-crap install +
  CRAP gate step to the `coverage` job.
- `CLAUDE.md` — short note: cargo-crap pinned to 0.2.2, blessed entrypoint `cargo xcrap` /
  `cargo xtask ci crap`, gate is `--fail-above --threshold 30`, allow-list lives in
  `crap.rs::ALLOW_GLOBS` with justifications. Mirror the existing cargo-mutants version-pin note.

## Reuse (do not reinvent)

- `xtask/src/mutants.rs` — copy its `Options`/`run`/`preflight`/`Error`/version-const/test shape.
- `ci::cargo()` helper (`mod.rs:858`) for spawning — but crap is a non-`cargo`-builtin tool invoked
  as `cargo crap`, so `crap::run` spawns `Command::new("cargo").arg("crap")...` with inherited
  stdio (same as `mutants::run`), not `ci::cargo` (which sets `Step`-typed errors).
- `coverage::*` already produces `lcov.info`; crap just reads it.

## Verification

1. `cargo test -p xtask` — inline `ci::tests` (pipeline contains `Crap` after `Coverage`, excludes
   it from fast, no `Check` anywhere) and `crap::tests` (version pin, `build_args`, format-for-env)
   all pass.
2. `cargo build -p xtask` — zero warnings (missing_docs etc.).
3. `cargo xcrap --help` and `cargo xtask ci crap` resolve and run.
4. **Calibration (A5):** real `cargo xci coverage` → `lcov.info` → `cargo xtask ci crap` exits 0
   with the seeded excludes (or surfaces offenders for a joint decision). This is the gate's
   acceptance test and must pass before the PR.
5. `cargo xci-fast` passes (confirms `check` removal didn't break the fast pipeline; confirms crap
   is correctly absent from fast).
6. Manually trace `ci.yml` `needs:` graph: no job references the deleted `check`; no cycles; every
   former `[unit]` dependent now `[clippy]`.
7. Push PR (with `Closes #<issue>` if issue-backed), note the branch-protection follow-up (B3) in
   the body, run the mandatory `post-implementation-audit` and `waiting-for-codex-review` skills per
   CLAUDE.md.

  into `ci::Error` (extend `ci::Error` with a `Crap(crap::Error)` arm + `Display`, mirroring the
  existing `Coverage`/`Lint` arms).
- Add `Step::Crap` to `PIPELINE_ORDER` **immediately after `Step::Coverage`** (`mod.rs:138`) so it
  reads the freshly merged lcov. **Do not** add to `FAST_PIPELINE_ORDER` (fast skips coverage → no
  lcov).

### A3. Update inline tests + docs in `ci/mod.rs` and `main.rs`

- `step_name_covers_all_variants` (`mod.rs:1418`): add `Step::Crap` to the `all` array.
- Add a test: `Step::Crap` is in `PIPELINE_ORDER`, sits directly after `Step::Coverage`, and is
  **absent** from `FAST_PIPELINE_ORDER` (add `Step::Crap` to the `banned` list in
  `fast_pipeline_excludes_*` since it requires coverage's lcov).
- New dev entrypoint: add a `Crap { lcov: Option<PathBuf>, threshold: Option<u32> }` subcommand in
  `main.rs` → `crap::run(... format: Human ...)` (defaults `lcov.info`, `CRAP_THRESHOLD`), and a
  `xcrap = "xtask crap"` alias in `.cargo/config.toml`. Lets contributors reproduce the gate
  locally with readable output after a coverage run (parallels the `xclippy` dev vs `ci clippy`
  split).
- Update the "all 20 gates" prose (`main.rs:20`, `mod.rs:103`) and the `cargo xci check clippy`
  example in the module header (`mod.rs:5`) to drop `check` and mention `crap`.

### A4. CI: append cargo-crap to the existing `coverage` job (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`)

In the `coverage` job (lines 218-288), after the `Coverage` step (line 269):
- Add `- uses: taiki-e/install-action@v2` with `tool: cargo-crap@0.2.2` (pinned; taiki-e falls
  back to cargo-binstall, which the blog confirms supports cargo-crap). Place it near the other
  `install-action` steps.
- Add `- name: CRAP gate \n run: cargo xtask ci crap`. It fails the job on threshold breach
  (`--fail-above`) and emits `::warning` annotations (github format via `format_for_env`). The
  existing `if: always()` Codecov upload still runs even if CRAP fails.

### A5. Calibration pass (MANDATORY during implementation, before locking the gate)

The full coverage job can't run in plan mode. During implementation:
1. Run `cargo xci coverage` locally (or the native-only subset) to produce a real `lcov.info`.
2. Run `cargo crap --workspace --lcov lcov.info --threshold 30 --format human --top 40` and inspect.
3. For every function over 30: prefer **adding tests** (lowers coverage-driven CRAP, matches the
   repo's test-first ethos); otherwise add a justified `EXCLUDE_GLOBS`/`ALLOW_GLOBS` entry. Only
   touch `CRAP_THRESHOLD` as a last resort.
4. **Surface the calibration result to the user** before finalizing — if real code can't pass 30
   cleanly, we decide together (more tests vs. allow-list vs. threshold) rather than silently
   widening the gate.

## Part B — CI speedup + remove `check`

### B1. Remove `check` from xtask

- Delete `Step::Check` variant (`mod.rs:37`), `step_name` arm (`mod.rs:1210`), `run_step` arm
  (`mod.rs:397`), `fn run_check` (`mod.rs:458`), and the `Step::Check` entry in `PIPELINE_ORDER`.
- Fix `explicit_steps_pass_through` test (`mod.rs:1287`, uses `Step::Check`) — switch to e.g.
  `[Step::Clippy, Step::Unit]`. Remove `Step::Check` from `step_name_covers_all_variants`.
- `cargo_with_format` stays (clippy still uses it). No other `run_check`-only code remains.

### B2. `.github/workflows/ci.yml` rewiring

- **Delete the `check` job** (lines 51-61).
- `unit.needs`: `[check, clippy]` → `[clippy]`.
- `mutual-exclusion.needs`: `[check]` → `[clippy]`.
- Rewire these jobs from `needs: [unit]` → `needs: [clippy]` (clippy guarantees compilation; none
  consume unit-test *results*): `i18n-browser`, `dom-browser`, `release`, `integration`, `adapter`,
  `error-variant-coverage`, `coverage`, `feature-flags-{core,i18n,subsystems,leptos,dioxus}`.
- `adapter-parity.needs`: `[unit, adapter]` → `[clippy]` (it only counts test files on disk).
- Net graph: `fmt → clippy → { unit, release, integration, adapter, adapter-parity,
  error-variant-coverage, mutual-exclusion, i18n-browser, dom-browser, coverage(+crap),
  feature-flags-* → feature-flag-check } `. Removes one full compile stage from the critical path.
- `feature-flag-check` aggregator unchanged.

### B3. Required manual follow-up (flag to user; cannot be done in-repo)

Removing the `check` job means GitHub **branch protection → required status checks** must drop
`Workspace Check` (job name "Workspace Check"), or PR merges will block waiting on a job that no
longer reports. This is a repo-admin action in GitHub settings, not a file change. Call it out in
the PR description.

## Critical files

- `xtask/src/crap.rs` — **new** module (mirror `xtask/src/mutants.rs`).
- `xtask/src/lib.rs`, `xtask/src/main.rs` — register module, add `Crap` subcommand, update prose.
- `xtask/src/ci/mod.rs` — add `Step::Crap` + `run_crap` + `Error::Crap`; remove `Step::Check` +
  `run_check`; update `PIPELINE_ORDER`, `step_name`, `run_step`, and the inline `mod tests`.
- `.cargo/config.toml` — add `xcrap = "xtask crap"`.
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — remove `check` job; rewire `needs:`; append cargo-crap install +
  CRAP gate step to the `coverage` job.
- `CLAUDE.md` — short note: cargo-crap pinned to 0.2.2, blessed entrypoint `cargo xcrap` /
  `cargo xtask ci crap`, gate is `--fail-above --threshold 30`, allow-list lives in
  `crap.rs::ALLOW_GLOBS` with justifications. Mirror the existing cargo-mutants version-pin note.

## Reuse (do not reinvent)

- `xtask/src/mutants.rs` — copy its `Options`/`run`/`preflight`/`Error`/version-const/test shape.
- `ci::cargo()` helper (`mod.rs:858`) for spawning — but crap is a non-`cargo`-builtin tool invoked
  as `cargo crap`, so `crap::run` spawns `Command::new("cargo").arg("crap")...` with inherited
  stdio (same as `mutants::run`), not `ci::cargo` (which sets `Step`-typed errors).
- `coverage::*` already produces `lcov.info`; crap just reads it.

## Verification

1. `cargo test -p xtask` — inline `ci::tests` (pipeline contains `Crap` after `Coverage`, excludes
   it from fast, no `Check` anywhere) and `crap::tests` (version pin, `build_args`, format-for-env)
   all pass.
2. `cargo build -p xtask` — zero warnings (missing_docs etc.).
3. `cargo xcrap --help` and `cargo xtask ci crap` resolve and run.
4. **Calibration (A5):** real `cargo xci coverage` → `lcov.info` → `cargo xtask ci crap` exits 0
   with the seeded excludes (or surfaces offenders for a joint decision). This is the gate's
   acceptance test and must pass before the PR.
5. `cargo xci-fast` passes (confirms `check` removal didn't break the fast pipeline; confirms crap
   is correctly absent from fast).
6. Manually trace `ci.yml` `needs:` graph: no job references the deleted `check`; no cycles; every
   former `[unit]` dependent now `[clippy]`.
7. Push PR (with `Closes #<issue>` if issue-backed), note the branch-protection follow-up (B3) in
   the body, run the mandatory `post-implementation-audit` and `waiting-for-codex-review` skills per
   CLAUDE.md.

content.text:
The plan is written. Here's the summary.

## Plan: integrate cargo-crap + speed up CI

**cargo-crap (hard-fail gate):**
- New `xtask/src/crap.rs` modeled on the existing `mutants.rs` — pinned `CARGO_CRAP_VERSION = "0.2.2"`, `CRAP_THRESHOLD = 30`, testable `build_args`, preflight, `EXCLUDE_GLOBS`/`ALLOW_GLOBS` (justified-escape-hatch list), and `format_for_env` so CI gets `::warning` annotations while local output stays human-readable.
- New `Step::Crap` placed right after `Step::Coverage` in the full pipeline (it reuses the merged `lcov.info` that `run_coverage` already writes — **zero recompile**), excluded from the fast pipeline. Appended as a step in the existing `coverage` CI job rather than a separate job.
- Dev entrypoint `cargo xcrap` for local reproduction.

**CI cleanup (your confirmed choices):**
- Remove `check` entirely (clippy `--all-targets` is a strict superset) — variant, `run_check`, CI job, and dependents rewired.
- Moderate speedup: independent compile jobs move from `needs: [unit]` to `needs: [clippy]`, removing a full compile stage from the critical path.

**Two things I flagged that need your awareness:**
1. **Calibration is mandatory before the gate locks.** `cargo crap --workspace` scores every member against `lcov.info`; `ars-derive` (unmeasured proc-macro) would falsely explode, so it's seeded into `EXCLUDE_GLOBS`. I can't run the ~90-min coverage job in plan mode, so during implementation I'll do a real coverage→crap pass and surface any over-threshold functions to you before finalizing (tests preferred over allow-list over threshold change).
2. **Branch protection** must drop the "Workspace Check" required status after removing the `check` job — a GitHub-settings action only you (admin) can do, or merges will hang. Called out in the plan and to go in the PR body.

input.plan:
# Integrate cargo-crap + improve CI

## Context

We want two things in one effort:

1. **Integrate [`cargo-crap`](https://minikin.me/blog/cargo-crap)** (the CRAP metric: cyclomatic
   complexity vs. test coverage) into the xtask CI engine and GitHub Actions. cargo-crap surfaces
   functions that are both complex and undertested — the highest-risk code to modify, especially
   under AI-assisted development. It is already installed locally at `~/.cargo/bin/cargo-crap`
   (v0.2.2).

2. **Speed up CI and remove redundancy.** The `check` job is redundant — `clippy --workspace
   --all-targets --all-features` compiles a strict superset of what `cargo check` compiles, plus
   lints. And the whole pipeline serializes behind `fmt → clippy → unit` before fanning out, even
   though most fan-out jobs don't consume unit-test *results* (clippy already guarantees the code
   compiles).

**Decisions taken (user-confirmed):**
- cargo-crap gates CI with a **hard fail**: `cargo crap --workspace --lcov lcov.info --fail-above
  --threshold 30`.
- **Remove `check` entirely** (variant, `run_check`, CI job, pipeline entries; rewire dependents).
- **Moderate CI speedup**: rewire independent compile jobs from `needs: [unit]` to `needs:
  [clippy]` so they run in parallel with `unit` instead of waiting for it.

**Key facts established during exploration:**
- xtask is the single source of truth for CI steps (`xtask/src/ci/mod.rs`). CI YAML just calls
  `cargo xtask ci <step>`.
- The `coverage` step (`run_coverage`, `mod.rs:730`) already writes the merged native+wasm
  `lcov.info` to the repo root (`mod.rs:743,782`). cargo-crap consumes exactly that file and needs
  **no recompile** — it parses source + reads lcov. So cargo-crap belongs right after `Coverage`
  in the pipeline and as an appended step in the existing `coverage` CI job (zero extra compile,
  no artifact round-trip).
- `cargo-crap` v0.2.2 takes options directly (`cargo crap [OPTIONS]`, no `crap` subcommand);
  `--fail-above` controls exit code independently of `--format`; `--format github` emits
  `::warning` annotations; it auto-detects `--repo-url`/`--commit-ref` from `GITHUB_SERVER_URL`/
  `GITHUB_REPOSITORY`/`GITHUB_SHA`.
- The existing tool-integration template to mirror is `xtask/src/mutants.rs` (pinned-version const,
  `Options` struct, `preflight_*` check, `MissingTool` error, unit tests asserting the version pin
  and argument construction).
- `ci/mod.rs` has a thorough inline test suite (`mod tests`, ~line 1275) asserting pipeline
  contents, `step_name` coverage, and fast-vs-full invariants. These tests must be updated in lockstep.

## Part A — cargo-crap integration

### A1. New module `xtask/src/crap.rs` (mirror `mutants.rs`)

Centralize all gate config here as testable constants (single source of truth, same pattern as
`CARGO_MUTANTS_VERSION` / `LEPTOSFMT_VERSION`):

- `pub const CARGO_CRAP_VERSION: &str = "0.2.2";` — keep in sync with `ci.yml` and CLAUDE.md.
- `pub const CRAP_THRESHOLD: u32 = 30;` — the fail-above threshold.
- `const EXCLUDE_GLOBS: &[&str]` — files skipped at walk time. **Seed with** `crates/ars-derive/**`
  (proc-macro crate, intentionally unmeasured by llvm-cov; would otherwise score 0% coverage and
  explode CRAP). Add `target/**` defensively. Each entry carries a justification comment.
- `const ALLOW_GLOBS: &[&str]` — escape hatch for genuinely-justified high-CRAP functions
  discovered during calibration (A4). Empty initially. Each entry **must** have a comment
  explaining why (matches the repo's "named escape hatch + justification" convention, cf.
  `mutants.toml` `[[skip]]`).
- `pub enum Format { Human, Github }` + `fn format_for_env() -> Format` reading `GITHUB_ACTIONS`
  (set by Actions) → `Github`, else `Human`. Keeps CI annotations without polluting local output.
- `pub struct Options { lcov: PathBuf, threshold: u32, fail_above: bool, format: Format }`.
- `fn build_args(opts: &Options) -> Vec<String>` — **pure, testable** arg builder producing
  `["crap", "--workspace", "--lcov", <lcov>, "--threshold", <n>, "--format", <f>, ("--fail-above"),
  "--exclude", <g>..., "--allow", <g>...]`.
- `pub fn run(opts: &Options) -> Result<(), Error>` — `preflight_cargo_crap()`, then preflight that
  `opts.lcov` exists (helpful error pointing at `cargo xci coverage`), spawn `cargo` with
  `build_args`, inherit stdio, map exit code.
- `preflight_cargo_crap()` — `cargo crap --version` succeeds, else `Error::MissingTool` with hint
  `cargo install cargo-crap --locked --version {CARGO_CRAP_VERSION}`.
- `Error` enum + `Display` + `From<io::Error>` — copy the shape from `mutants.rs`.
- `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`: assert `CARGO_CRAP_VERSION == "0.2.2"`; assert `build_args` includes
  `--workspace`, `--fail-above` when set and omits it when not, the threshold, every `EXCLUDE_GLOBS`
  entry as an `--exclude`, every `ALLOW_GLOBS` entry as an `--allow`; assert `format_for_env` maps
  correctly given an injected env value (refactor to take the var so it's testable).

Register `mod crap;` in `xtask/src/lib.rs` and `main.rs`.

### A2. Wire `Step::Crap` into `xtask/src/ci/mod.rs`

- Add `Step::Crap` variant (doc: "Run cargo-crap CRAP gate over the merged lcov.info").

  crap::CRAP_THRESHOLD, fail_above: true, format: crap::format_for_env() })`, mapping `crap::Error`
  into `ci::Error` (extend `ci::Error` with a `Crap(crap::Error)` arm + `Display`, mirroring the
  existing `Coverage`/`Lint` arms).
- Add `Step::Crap` to `PIPELINE_ORDER` **immediately after `Step::Coverage`** (`mod.rs:138`) so it
  reads the freshly merged lcov. **Do not** add to `FAST_PIPELINE_ORDER` (fast skips coverage → no
  lcov).

### A3. Update inline tests + docs in `ci/mod.rs` and `main.rs`

- `step_name_covers_all_variants` (`mod.rs:1418`): add `Step::Crap` to the `all` array.
- Add a test: `Step::Crap` is in `PIPELINE_ORDER`, sits directly after `Step::Coverage`, and is
  **absent** from `FAST_PIPELINE_ORDER` (add `Step::Crap` to the `banned` list in
  `fast_pipeline_excludes_*` since it requires coverage's lcov).
- New dev entrypoint: add a `Crap { lcov: Option<PathBuf>, threshold: Option<u32> }` subcommand in
  `main.rs` → `crap::run(... format: Human ...)` (defaults `lcov.info`, `CRAP_THRESHOLD`), and a
  `xcrap = "xtask crap"` alias in `.cargo/config.toml`. Lets contributors reproduce the gate
  locally with readable output after a coverage run (parallels the `xclippy` dev vs `ci clippy`
  split).
- Update the "all 20 gates" prose (`main.rs:20`, `mod.rs:103`) and the `cargo xci check clippy`
  example in the module header (`mod.rs:5`) to drop `check` and mention `crap`.

### A4. CI: append cargo-crap to the existing `coverage` job (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`)

In the `coverage` job (lines 218-288), after the `Coverage` step (line 269):
- Add `- uses: taiki-e/install-action@v2` with `tool: cargo-crap@0.2.2` (pinned; taiki-e falls
  back to cargo-binstall, which the blog confirms supports cargo-crap). Place it near the other
  `install-action` steps.
- Add `- name: CRAP gate \n run: cargo xtask ci crap`. It fails the job on threshold breach
  (`--fail-above`) and emits `::warning` annotations (github format via `format_for_env`). The
  existing `if: always()` Codecov upload still runs even if CRAP fails.

### A5. Calibration pass (MANDATORY during implementation, before locking the gate)

The full coverage job can't run in plan mode. During implementation:
1. Run `cargo xci coverage` locally (or the native-only subset) to produce a real `lcov.info`.
2. Run `cargo crap --workspace --lcov lcov.info --threshold 30 --format human --top 40` and inspect.
3. For every function over 30: prefer **adding tests** (lowers coverage-driven CRAP, matches the
   repo's test-first ethos); otherwise add a justified `EXCLUDE_GLOBS`/`ALLOW_GLOBS` entry. Only
   touch `CRAP_THRESHOLD` as a last resort.
4. **Surface the calibration result to the user** before finalizing — if real code can't pass 30
   cleanly, we decide together (more tests vs. allow-list vs. threshold) rather than silently
   widening the gate.

## Part B — CI speedup + remove `check`

### B1. Remove `check` from xtask

- Delete `Step::Check` variant (`mod.rs:37`), `step_name` arm (`mod.rs:1210`), `run_step` arm
  (`mod.rs:397`), `fn run_check` (`mod.rs:458`), and the `Step::Check` entry in `PIPELINE_ORDER`.
- Fix `explicit_steps_pass_through` test (`mod.rs:1287`, uses `Step::Check`) — switch to e.g.
  `[Step::Clippy, Step::Unit]`. Remove `Step::Check` from `step_name_covers_all_variants`.
- `cargo_with_format` stays (clippy still uses it). No other `run_check`-only code remains.

### B2. `.github/workflows/ci.yml` rewiring

- **Delete the `check` job** (lines 51-61).
- `unit.needs`: `[check, clippy]` → `[clippy]`.
- `mutual-exclusion.needs`: `[check]` → `[clippy]`.
- Rewire these jobs from `needs: [unit]` → `needs: [clippy]` (clippy guarantees compilation; none
  consume unit-test *results*): `i18n-browser`, `dom-browser`, `release`, `integration`, `adapter`,
  `error-variant-coverage`, `coverage`, `feature-flags-{core,i18n,subsystems,leptos,dioxus}`.
- `adapter-parity.needs`: `[unit, adapter]` → `[clippy]` (it only counts test files on disk).
- Net graph: `fmt → clippy → { unit, release, integration, adapter, adapter-parity,
  error-variant-coverage, mutual-exclusion, i18n-browser, dom-browser, coverage(+crap),
  feature-flags-* → feature-flag-check } `. Removes one full compile stage from the critical path.
- `feature-flag-check` aggregator unchanged.

### B3. Required manual follow-up (flag to user; cannot be done in-repo)

Removing the `check` job means GitHub **branch protection → required status checks** must drop

longer reports. This is a repo-admin action in GitHub settings, not a file change. Call it out in
the PR description.

## Critical files

- `xtask/src/crap.rs` — **new** module (mirror `xtask/src/mutants.rs`).
- `xtask/src/lib.rs`, `xtask/src/main.rs` — register module, add `Crap` subcommand, update prose.
- `xtask/src/ci/mod.rs` — add `Step::Crap` + `run_crap` + `Error::Crap`; remove `Step::Check` +

- `xtask/src/mutants.rs` — copy its `Options`/`run`/`preflight`/`Error`/version-const/test shape.
- `ci::cargo()` helper (`mod.rs:858`) for spawning — but crap is a non-`cargo`-builtin tool invoked
  as `cargo crap`, so `crap::run` spawns `Command::new("cargo").arg("crap")...` with inherited

content.text:
Good question — and the answer is **keep `unit`**. They overlap in test *execution* but serve different purposes, and after the moderate rewiring they're no longer serially redundant.

What each actually does:

| | `unit` job | `coverage` job |
|---|---|---|
| Command | `cargo xtest unit` + `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` | `cargo +nightly llvm-cov nextest --branch --workspace` + wasm coverage + threshold check (+ crap) |
| Toolchain | **stable** (what users actually build with) | **nightly** (llvm-cov `--branch` needs it) |
| Speed | fast, no instrumentation | the ~90-min tail |
| Snapshot gate | **yes** (`--unreferenced=reject`) | no |

Why dropping `unit` would be a regression:

1. **Feedback latency.** `coverage` is the slowest job in the pipeline. If it's the only thing running tests, every test break now takes ~90 min to surface instead of a few minutes. `unit` is the fast fail signal.
2. **Stable vs nightly.** `unit` runs the suite on the stable toolchain — the real correctness signal for the toolchain users ship on. `coverage` runs on nightly purely to get instrumentation. A divergence between them is exactly the kind of thing you want stable CI to catch.
3. **Snapshot enforcement.** `unit` also runs `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject`, which `coverage` doesn't. That gate would be lost.

On the redundancy you're sensing: in the *current* graph it's real-ish — `coverage` runs `needs: [unit]`, so it executes the tests a *second* time, serially, after `unit` already ran them. My moderate rewiring fixes exactly that: both become `needs: [clippy]` and run **in parallel**. The suite still executes twice, but concurrently on separate runners, so it costs runner-minutes, not wall-clock — and you keep the fast stable signal + snapshot gate while coverage does its slow instrumented measurement alongside.

So no plan change needed — the rewiring already converts the serial double-run into a parallel one, which is the win here. If you'd instead prefer to *reduce runner cost* by having `coverage` not duplicate the native test run, that's a different (and fiddlier) optimization we could fold in, but it trades feedback latency for runner-minutes and I wouldn't recommend it.

Want me to proceed with the plan as written, or fold in that runner-cost optimization first?

=== RUST SKILLS DISPLAY FORMAT ===
When showing Rust Skills loaded, display in this EXACT order:
1. FIRST: "🦀 Rust Skills Loaded" text
2. THEN: The Ferris crab ASCII art BELOW the text
The text must be ABOVE the crab, not below.
===

=== MANDATORY: META-COGNITION ROUTING ===

CRITICAL: You MUST follow the COMPLETE meta-cognition framework.
Partial compliance (only loading L1 skill) is NOT acceptable.

## STEP 1: IDENTIFY ENTRY LAYER + DOMAIN

### Layer 1 Signals (Start here, trace UP):
- Error codes: E0382, E0597, E0277, E0499, etc.
- Keywords: cannot be sent, moved value, borrowed, lifetime

### Layer 3 Domain Signals (MUST also load domain skill):

| Keywords in Question | Domain Skill to Load |
|---------------------|---------------------|
| Web API, HTTP, REST, axum, actix, handler, router | domain-web |
| payment, trading, fintech, decimal, currency | domain-fintech |
| CLI, command line, clap, terminal | domain-cli |
| embedded, no_std, MCU, firmware | domain-embedded |
| kubernetes, docker, grpc, microservice | domain-cloud-native |
| MQTT, sensor, IoT, telemetry | domain-iot |
| tensor, model, inference, ML | domain-ml |

**CRITICAL**: If domain keywords are present, you MUST load BOTH:
1. The Layer 1 skill (e.g., m07-concurrency)
2. The Layer 3 domain skill (e.g., domain-web)

## STEP 2: EXECUTE TRACING (MANDATORY)

For Layer 1 entry with domain context:

```
L1 Error (e.g., Rc not Send)
    ^ TRACE UP: Why this design constraint?
    ^ Load domain skill (e.g., domain-web)
    ^ Find domain constraint (e.g., "handlers run on any thread")
    v TRACE DOWN: What pattern satisfies constraint?
    v Return to L1 with context-aware solution
```

## STEP 3: MANDATORY OUTPUT FORMAT

Your response MUST include ALL of these sections:

### Reasoning Chain
```
+-- Layer 1: [specific error]
|   Problem: [error description]
|       ^
+-- Layer 3: [domain name] (domain-xxx)
|   Constraint: [what is the domain constraint]
|   Rule: [why this constraint exists]
|       v
+-- Layer 2: Design Choice
    Decision: [design decision based on domain constraint]
```

### Domain Constraints Analysis
- MUST reference specific rules from domain-xxx skill
- Explain WHY this domain has this constraint

### Recommended Solution
- Provide solution that follows domain best practices
- Not just fixing the compile error

## EXAMPLE: Web API + Send Error

Question: "Web API config sharing error: Rc cannot be sent"

CORRECT Response:
```
### Reasoning Chain
+-- Layer 1: Send/Sync Error
|   Problem: Rc<T> cannot be sent between threads
|       ^
+-- Layer 3: Web Domain (domain-web)
|   Constraint: Handlers run on any thread
|   Rule: Shared state must be thread-safe
|       v
+-- Layer 2: Design Choice
    Decision: Use Arc<T> + State extractor

### Domain Constraints Analysis
From domain-web:
- "Rc in state" is a Common Mistake
- Web handlers require Send + Sync
- Recommended: axum State<Arc<T>> pattern

### Recommended Solution
[Code following Web best practices]
```

WRONG Response (stops at L1):
```
Problem: Rc is not Send
Solution: Use Arc
```

## SKILLS TO INVOKE

Always invoke with Skill() tool:
- Skill(rust-router) - First, to get routing
- Skill(m0x-xxx) - Layer 1 skill based on error
- Skill(domain-xxx) - Layer 3 skill based on domain keywords

===================================

input.plan:
# Integrate cargo-crap + improve CI

## Context

We want two things in one effort:

1. **Integrate [`cargo-crap`](https://minikin.me/blog/cargo-crap)** (the CRAP metric: cyclomatic
   complexity vs. test coverage) into the xtask CI engine and GitHub Actions. cargo-crap surfaces
   functions that are both complex and undertested — the highest-risk code to modify, especially
   under AI-assisted development. It is already installed locally at `~/.cargo/bin/cargo-crap`
   (v0.2.2).

2. **Speed up CI and remove redundancy.** The `check` job is redundant — `clippy --workspace
   --all-targets --all-features` compiles a strict superset of what `cargo check` compiles, plus
   lints. And the whole pipeline serializes behind `fmt → clippy → unit` before fanning out, even
   though most fan-out jobs don't consume unit-test *results* (clippy already guarantees the code
   compiles).

**Decisions taken (user-confirmed):**
- cargo-crap gates CI with a **hard fail**: `cargo crap --workspace --lcov lcov.info --fail-above
  --threshold 30`.
- **Remove `check` entirely** (variant, `run_check`, CI job, pipeline entries; rewire dependents).
- **Moderate CI speedup**: rewire independent compile jobs from `needs: [unit]` to `needs:
  [clippy]` so they run in parallel with `unit` instead of waiting for it.

**Key facts established during exploration:**
- xtask is the single source of truth for CI steps (`xtask/src/ci/mod.rs`). CI YAML just calls
  `cargo xtask ci <step>`.
- The `coverage` step (`run_coverage`, `mod.rs:730`) already writes the merged native+wasm
  `lcov.info` to the repo root (`mod.rs:743,782`). cargo-crap consumes exactly that file and needs
  **no recompile** — it parses source + reads lcov. So cargo-crap belongs right after `Coverage`
  in the pipeline and as an appended step in the existing `coverage` CI job (zero extra compile,
  no artifact round-trip).
- `cargo-crap` v0.2.2 takes options directly (`cargo crap [OPTIONS]`, no `crap` subcommand);
  `--fail-above` controls exit code independently of `--format`; `--format github` emits
  `::warning` annotations; it auto-detects `--repo-url`/`--commit-ref` from `GITHUB_SERVER_URL`/
  `GITHUB_REPOSITORY`/`GITHUB_SHA`.
- The existing tool-integration template to mirror is `xtask/src/mutants.rs` (pinned-version const,
  `Options` struct, `preflight_*` check, `MissingTool` error, unit tests asserting the version pin
  and argument construction).
- `ci/mod.rs` has a thorough inline test suite (`mod tests`, ~line 1275) asserting pipeline
  contents, `step_name` coverage, and fast-vs-full invariants. These tests must be updated in lockstep.

## Part A — cargo-crap integration

### A1. New module `xtask/src/crap.rs` (mirror `mutants.rs`)

Centralize all gate config here as testable constants (single source of truth, same pattern as
`CARGO_MUTANTS_VERSION` / `LEPTOSFMT_VERSION`):

- `pub const CARGO_CRAP_VERSION: &str = "0.2.2";` — keep in sync with `ci.yml` and CLAUDE.md.
- `pub const CRAP_THRESHOLD: u32 = 30;` — the fail-above threshold.
- `const EXCLUDE_GLOBS: &[&str]` — files skipped at walk time. **Seed with** `crates/ars-derive/**`
  (proc-macro crate, intentionally unmeasured by llvm-cov; would otherwise score 0% coverage and
  explode CRAP). Add `target/**` defensively. Each entry carries a justification comment.
- `const ALLOW_GLOBS: &[&str]` — escape hatch for genuinely-justified high-CRAP functions
  discovered during calibration (A4). Empty initially. Each entry **must** have a comment
  explaining why (matches the repo's "named escape hatch + justification" convention, cf.
  `mutants.toml` `[[skip]]`).
- `pub enum Format { Human, Github }` + `fn format_for_env() -> Format` reading `GITHUB_ACTIONS`
  (set by Actions) → `Github`, else `Human`. Keeps CI annotations without polluting local output.
- `pub struct Options { lcov: PathBuf, threshold: u32, fail_above: bool, format: Format }`.
- `fn build_args(opts: &Options) -> Vec<String>` — **pure, testable** arg builder producing
  `["crap", "--workspace", "--lcov", <lcov>, "--threshold", <n>, "--format", <f>, ("--fail-above"),
  "--exclude", <g>..., "--allow", <g>...]`.
- `pub fn run(opts: &Options) -> Result<(), Error>` — `preflight_cargo_crap()`, then preflight that
  `opts.lcov` exists (helpful error pointing at `cargo xci coverage`), spawn `cargo` with
  `build_args`, inherit stdio, map exit code.
- `preflight_cargo_crap()` — `cargo crap --version` succeeds, else `Error::MissingTool` with hint
  `cargo install cargo-crap --locked --version {CARGO_CRAP_VERSION}`.
- `Error` enum + `Display` + `From<io::Error>` — copy the shape from `mutants.rs`.
- `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`: assert `CARGO_CRAP_VERSION == "0.2.2"`; assert `build_args` includes
  `--workspace`, `--fail-above` when set and omits it when not, the threshold, every `EXCLUDE_GLOBS`
  entry as an `--exclude`, every `ALLOW_GLOBS` entry as an `--allow`; assert `format_for_env` maps
  correctly given an injected env value (refactor to take the var so it's testable).

Register `mod crap;` in `xtask/src/lib.rs` and `main.rs`.

### A2. Wire `Step::Crap` into `xtask/src/ci/mod.rs`

- Add `Step::Crap` variant (doc: "Run cargo-crap CRAP gate over the merged lcov.info").
- `step_name`: `Step::Crap => "crap"` (`mod.rs:1207`).
- `run_step`: `Step::Crap => run_crap()` (`mod.rs:393`).
- `fn run_crap()` → `crap::run(&crap::Options { lcov: "lcov.info".into(), threshold:
  crap::CRAP_THRESHOLD, fail_above: true, format: crap::format_for_env() })`, mapping `crap::Error`
  into `ci::Error` (extend `ci::Error` with a `Crap(crap::Error)` arm + `Display`, mirroring the
  existing `Coverage`/`Lint` arms).
- Add `Step::Crap` to `PIPELINE_ORDER` **immediately after `Step::Coverage`** (`mod.rs:138`) so it
  reads the freshly merged lcov. **Do not** add to `FAST_PIPELINE_ORDER` (fast skips coverage → no
  lcov).

### A3. Update inline tests + docs in `ci/mod.rs` and `main.rs`

- `step_name_covers_all_variants` (`mod.rs:1418`): add `Step::Crap` to the `all` array.
- Add a test: `Step::Crap` is in `PIPELINE_ORDER`, sits directly after `Step::Coverage`, and is
  **absent** from `FAST_PIPELINE_ORDER` (add `Step::Crap` to the `banned` list in
  `fast_pipeline_excludes_*` since it requires coverage's lcov).
- New dev entrypoint: add a `Crap { lcov: Option<PathBuf>, threshold: Option<u32> }` subcommand in
  `main.rs` → `crap::run(... format: Human ...)` (defaults `lcov.info`, `CRAP_THRESHOLD`), and a
  `xcrap = "xtask crap"` alias in `.cargo/config.toml`. Lets contributors reproduce the gate
  locally with readable output after a coverage run (parallels the `xclippy` dev vs `ci clippy`
  split).
- Update the "all 20 gates" prose (`main.rs:20`, `mod.rs:103`) and the `cargo xci check clippy`
  example in the module header (`mod.rs:5`) to drop `check` and mention `crap`.

### A4. CI: append cargo-crap to the existing `coverage` job (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`)

In the `coverage` job (lines 218-288), after the `Coverage` step (line 269):
- Add `- uses: taiki-e/install-action@v2` with `tool: cargo-crap@0.2.2` (pinned; taiki-e falls
  back to cargo-binstall, which the blog confirms supports cargo-crap). Place it near the other
  `install-action` steps.
- Add `- name: CRAP gate \n run: cargo xtask ci crap`. It fails the job on threshold breach
  (`--fail-above`) and emits `::warning` annotations (github format via `format_for_env`). The
  existing `if: always()` Codecov upload still runs even if CRAP fails.

### A5. Calibration pass (MANDATORY during implementation, before locking the gate)

The full coverage job can't run in plan mode. During implementation:
1. Run `cargo xci coverage` locally (or the native-only subset) to produce a real `lcov.info`.
2. Run `cargo crap --workspace --lcov lcov.info --threshold 30 --format human --top 40` and inspect.
3. For every function over 30: prefer **adding tests** (lowers coverage-driven CRAP, matches the
   repo's test-first ethos); otherwise add a justified `EXCLUDE_GLOBS`/`ALLOW_GLOBS` entry. Only
   touch `CRAP_THRESHOLD` as a last resort.
4. **Surface the calibration result to the user** before finalizing — if real code can't pass 30
   cleanly, we decide together (more tests vs. allow-list vs. threshold) rather than silently
   widening the gate.

## Part B — CI speedup + remove `check`

### B1. Remove `check` from xtask

- Delete `Step::Check` variant (`mod.rs:37`), `step_name` arm (`mod.rs:1210`), `run_step` arm
  (`mod.rs:397`), `fn run_check` (`mod.rs:458`), and the `Step::Check` entry in `PIPELINE_ORDER`.
- Fix `explicit_steps_pass_through` test (`mod.rs:1287`, uses `Step::Check`) — switch to e.g.
  `[Step::Clippy, Step::Unit]`. Remove `Step::Check` from `step_name_covers_all_variants`.
- `cargo_with_format` stays (clippy still uses it). No other `run_check`-only code remains.

### B2. `.github/workflows/ci.yml` rewiring

- **Delete the `check` job** (lines 51-61).
- `unit.needs`: `[check, clippy]` → `[clippy]`.
- `mutual-exclusion.needs`: `[check]` → `[clippy]`.
- Rewire these jobs from `needs: [unit]` → `needs: [clippy]` (clippy guarantees compilation; none
  consume unit-test *results*): `i18n-browser`, `dom-browser`, `release`, `integration`, `adapter`,
  `error-variant-coverage`, `coverage`, `feature-flags-{core,i18n,subsystems,leptos,dioxus}`.
- `adapter-parity.needs`: `[unit, adapter]` → `[clippy]` (it only counts test files on disk).
- Net graph: `fmt → clippy → { unit, release, integration, adapter, adapter-parity,
  error-variant-coverage, mutual-exclusion, i18n-browser, dom-browser, coverage(+crap),
  feature-flags-* → feature-flag-check } `. Removes one full compile stage from the critical path.
- `feature-flag-check` aggregator unchanged.

### B3. Required manual follow-up (flag to user; cannot be done in-repo)

Removing the `check` job means GitHub **branch protection → required status checks** must drop
`Workspace Check` (job name "Workspace Check"), or PR merges will block waiting on a job that no
longer reports. This is a repo-admin action in GitHub settings, not a file change. Call it out in
the PR description.

## Critical files

- `xtask/src/crap.rs` — **new** module (mirror `xtask/src/mutants.rs`).
- `xtask/src/lib.rs`, `xtask/src/main.rs` — register module, add `Crap` subcommand, update prose.
- `xtask/src/ci/mod.rs` — add `Step::Crap` + `run_crap` + `Error::Crap`; remove `Step::Check` +
  `run_check`; update `PIPELINE_ORDER`, `step_name`, `run_step`, and the inline `mod tests`.
- `.cargo/config.toml` — add `xcrap = "xtask crap"`.
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — remove `check` job; rewire `needs:`; append cargo-crap install +
  CRAP gate step to the `coverage` job.
- `CLAUDE.md` — short note: cargo-crap pinned to 0.2.2, blessed entrypoint `cargo xcrap` /
  `cargo xtask ci crap`, gate is `--fail-above --threshold 30`, allow-list lives in
  `crap.rs::ALLOW_GLOBS` with justifications. Mirror the existing cargo-mutants version-pin note.

## Reuse (do not reinvent)

- `xtask/src/mutants.rs` — copy its `Options`/`run`/`preflight`/`Error`/version-const/test shape.
- `ci::cargo()` helper (`mod.rs:858`) for spawning — but crap is a non-`cargo`-builtin tool invoked
  as `cargo crap`, so `crap::run` spawns `Command::new("cargo").arg("crap")...` with inherited
  stdio (same as `mutants::run`), not `ci::cargo` (which sets `Step`-typed errors).
- `coverage::*` already produces `lcov.info`; crap just reads it.

## Verification

1. `cargo test -p xtask` — inline `ci::tests` (pipeline contains `Crap` after `Coverage`, excludes
   it from fast, no `Check` anywhere) and `crap::tests` (version pin, `build_args`, format-for-env)
   all pass.
2. `cargo build -p xtask` — zero warnings (missing_docs etc.).
3. `cargo xcrap --help` and `cargo xtask ci crap` resolve and run.
4. **Calibration (A5):** real `cargo xci coverage` → `lcov.info` → `cargo xtask ci crap` exits 0
   with the seeded excludes (or surfaces offenders for a joint decision). This is the gate's
   acceptance test and must pass before the PR.
5. `cargo xci-fast` passes (confirms `check` removal didn't break the fast pipeline; confirms crap
   is correctly absent from fast).
6. Manually trace `ci.yml` `needs:` graph: no job references the deleted `check`; no cycles; every
   former `[unit]` dependent now `[clippy]`.
7. Push PR (with `Closes #<issue>` if issue-backed), note the branch-protection follow-up (B3) in
   the body, run the mandatory `post-implementation-audit` and `waiting-for-codex-review` skills per
   CLAUDE.md.

content.text:
Yes — 30 is the recommended starting threshold, on three grounds:

1. **It's cargo-crap's built-in default** (`--threshold` defaults to 30).
2. **It's the canonical CRAP cutoff** from the metric's origin (Crap4J, Savoia 2007), which is where the tool inherits it.
3. **The blog's own CI example uses `--threshold 30`.**

What 30 actually means, given `CRAP = complexity² · (1 − coverage)³ + complexity`:

| Function | CRAP | Verdict |
|---|---|---|
| complexity 30, **100% covered** | 30 | passes (boundary) |
| complexity 20, 80% covered | 23.2 | passes |
| complexity 10, 50% covered | 22.5 | passes |
| complexity 6, **0% covered** | 42 | **fails** |
| complexity 10, 0% covered | 110 | **fails** |

So it's lenient on well-tested code (full coverage tolerates up to ~30 branches) and strict on complex-but-untested code — exactly the risk profile we want to catch.

The one caveat for *this* repo: state-machine transition functions are match-heavy and can carry high cyclomatic complexity. But they're also heavily tested (74–77%+ coverage thresholds, proptest), which crushes the CRAP score back down. Whether 30 holds in practice is precisely what the **mandatory calibration pass** (A5) verifies against the real `lcov.info` — if a well-tested state machine needs headroom, we bump the threshold or add a justified allow-entry then, with real numbers, rather than guessing now.

So 30 stays as the documented starting point; calibration confirms or adjusts it. No plan change needed.

- cargo-crap gates CI with a **hard fail**: `cargo crap --workspace --lcov lcov.info --fail-above
  --threshold 30`.

1. **Integrate [`cargo-crap`](https://minikin.me/blog/cargo-crap)** (the CRAP metric: cyclomatic
   complexity vs. test coverage) into the xtask CI engine and GitHub Actions. cargo-crap surfaces
   functions that are both complex and undertested — the highest-risk code to modify, especially
   under AI-assisted development. It is already installed locally at `~/.cargo/bin/cargo-crap`
   (v0.2.2).

2. **Speed up CI and remove redundancy.** The `check` job is redundant — `clippy --workspace
   --all-targets --all-features` compiles a strict superset of what `cargo check` compiles, plus
   lints. And the whole pipeline serializes behind `fmt → clippy → unit` before fanning out, even
   though most fan-out jobs don't consume unit-test *results* (clippy already guarantees the code
   compiles).

  --threshold 30`.

  [clippy]` so they run in parallel with `unit` instead of waiting for it.
